Title: L-lysine metabolism to N-hydroxypipecolic acid: an integral immune-activating pathway in plants

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: activates systemic acquired resistance and immune amplification
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: foliage; locally inoculated and distal systemic leaves
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes pathogen-induced accumulation of Pip and NHP and supports SAR
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EDS1 strongly promotes pathogen-induced accumulation of Pip and NHP. 2. EDS1 acts upstream of NHP production and promotes expression of ALD1 and FMO1. 3. Pip/NHP in turn induces EDS1 expression as part of a defense amplification loop during SAR.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis; metabolite profiling; gene expression analysis
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pseudomonas syringae infection; SAR-inducing pathogen inoculation
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: ChIP-seq and transcriptomic findings referenced from prior studies; accession NA
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential upstream target to enhance NHP pathway activation and SAR, but broad immune signaling roles may cause pleiotropy.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: broad immune activation may reduce growth and photosynthetic capacity under SAR-associated states
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: classified here as Common Gene because it is an immune signaling node rather than pathway enzyme or transcription factor in this review.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: activates systemic acquired resistance and immune amplification
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: foliage; locally inoculated and distal systemic leaves
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes pathogen-induced Pip/NHP accumulation and is required for effective Pip-inducible resistance
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAD4 strongly promotes pathogen-induced Pip and NHP accumulation. 2. PAD4 acts upstream of NHP biosynthetic gene expression. 3. PAD4 is required for effective Pip-inducible resistance and participates in a positive defense amplification loop.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis; metabolite profiling; resistance assays; gene expression analysis
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pseudomonas syringae infection; SAR-inducing pathogen inoculation
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Referenced prior transcriptomic/genetic studies; accession NA
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible immune engineering target to boost NHP pathway and SAR, but likely pleiotropic because PAD4 controls broad defense networks.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: enhanced immune signaling may incur growth-defense tradeoffs
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: classified as Common Gene because it is an immune signaling component rather than a pathway enzyme or TF.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: activates systemic acquired resistance and immune amplification
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required for both SA- and Pip-triggered resistance responses downstream of the Pip/NHP pathway
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NPR1 is a convergence point for SA and Pip/NHP signaling in SAR. 2. npr1 mutants are compromised in both SA- and Pip-triggered resistance. 3. Direct binding of NHP to NPR1 is proposed as an open question, not established.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant resistance assays; prior biochemical characterization referenced
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key downstream signaling node for defense enhancement, but central SA pathway role makes trait engineering potentially costly.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: constitutive activation may impair growth due to defense-gene activation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as core signaling component summarized in this review, though not part of NHP biosynthesis itself.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: activates systemic acquired resistance and immune amplification
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required for proper resistance induction by exogenous SA and Pip, but not for SAR-related metabolite accumulation or defense gene expression
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MLO2 acts at a very downstream position of SA and Pip/NHP signaling during SAR. 2. It is required for biologically induced SAR and proper resistance induction by exogenous SA and Pip. 3. It does not control SAR-related metabolite accumulation or defense gene expression.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Represents a downstream immune output component, but direct value for NHP metabolic engineering is limited.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the review explicitly discusses its downstream role in Pip/NHP-mediated SAR.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: activates systemic acquired resistance; enhances defense readiness; primes immune responses; promotes HR
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: foliage; locally inoculated and distal systemic leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required for pathogen-induced biosynthesis of Pip and downstream NHP; ald1 mutants fail to accumulate Pip and are defective in SAR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ALD1 is the key aminotransferase initiating pathogen-inducible Pip/NHP biosynthesis from L-lysine. 2. ald1 knockout plants cannot generate Pip upon bacterial infection and are SAR-defective. 3. Biochemical studies demonstrated ALD1 catalyzes L-lysine α-transamination via the KAC pathway to dehydropipecolic intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: L-lysine to pipecolic acid to N-hydroxypipecolic acid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step; first committed biosynthetic step for pathogen-inducible Pip formation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: L-lysine; acceptor oxoacids such as pyruvate
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: ε-amino-α-ketocaproic acid (KAC), detected as cyclic dehydropipecolic acid intermediates (1,2-DP/2,3-DP)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: transfers the α-amino group of L-lysine to an acceptor oxoacid, generating KAC that spontaneously cyclizes to dehydropipecolic acid intermediates
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor pool for Pip and thereby for NHP
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: knockout analysis; recombinant enzyme assay; heterologous expression in E. coli; isotope labeling; GC-MS/LC-MS metabolite profiling; FTIR
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: strongly systemically induced in foliage after pathogen attack
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: pathogen inducible in inoculated and distal leaves
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolite profiling studies cited; omics accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for engineering broad-spectrum disease resistance; overexpression or ortholog deployment can elevate Pip/NHP pathway activity, though stacking with FMO1 and regulators may be superior.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: SAR-associated activation can correlate with growth/photosynthesis costs; specific ALD1 overexpression tradeoff not fully defined in this review
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also discussed in rice and clubmoss homologs; in vivo function appears specific for lysine-derived DP formation despite broader in vitro substrate range.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: activates systemic acquired resistance; enhances defense readiness; primes immune responses; promotes HR
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: foliage; locally inoculated and distal systemic leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: major reductase for conversion of dehydropipecolic intermediates to Pip; sard4 mutants show reduced Pip and elevated DP with compromised SAR-associated resistance
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SARD4 acts as a major dehydropipecolic acid reductase in pathogen-induced Pip biosynthesis. 2. sard4 knockout plants accumulate less Pip and overaccumulate DP intermediates after infection. 3. Residual Pip in sard4 mutants indicates additional reductase activities besides SARD4.
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: L-lysine to pipecolic acid to N-hydroxypipecolic acid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: major but not exclusive control step for DP-to-Pip reduction
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: reduces ALD1-generated dehydropipecolic acid intermediates to pipecolic acid using NAD(P)H
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: forward genetics; knockout analysis; coupled in vitro enzyme assays; heterologous co-expression in E. coli; metabolite profiling; isotopic analysis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: systemically induced in foliage upon bacterial attack
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: pathogen inducible in inoculated and distal leaves
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful engineering target to reinforce Pip supply, especially combined with ALD1 and FMO1; redundancy of additional reductases should be considered.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review notes other reductase activities beyond SARD4 likely contribute to Pip production.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: induces SAR, immune gene expression, defense priming, SA amplification and HR cell death promotion
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: foliage; locally inoculated and distal systemic leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: catalyzes conversion of Pip to NHP; fmo1 mutants fail to accumulate NHP and are defective in Pip-induced SAR, whereas FMO1 overexpression enhances resistance
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FMO1 is the Pip N-hydroxylase that synthesizes NHP, the active SAR-inducing metabolite. 2. fmo1 mutants are defective in NHP accumulation and Pip-induced resistance. 3. Overexpression of FMO1 enhances resistance, and recombinant or transiently expressed FMO1 converts Pip to NHP.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: flavin-containing monooxygenase; clade I FMO; FAD- and NAD(P)H-binding domains
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: L-lysine to pipecolic acid to N-hydroxypipecolic acid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: terminal N-hydroxylation step of NHP biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: terminal determinant for NHP formation and decisive control point for SAR activation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: N-hydroxylates L-pipecolic acid to produce N-hydroxypipecolic acid using FAD and NADH/NADPH with oxygen
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis; recombinant enzyme assay; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; isotope feeding; metabolite profiling; overexpression resistance assays
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: strongly systemically induced in foliage in response to pathogens
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: pathogen inducible in inoculated and distal leaves; correlated with senescence and DNA stress responses in additional contexts
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pseudomonas syringae infection; pathogen attack; zeocin-induced DNA damage; cell death-inducing stimuli
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for broad-spectrum disease resistance engineering; overexpression increases resistance, especially if coupled with upstream Pip-supply genes.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: enhanced immune activation may reduce growth/photosynthesis under sustained SAR-like states
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Subcellular localization remains unclear in this review; NHP-hexose accumulation is also FMO1 dependent.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: activates systemic acquired resistance and associated immune transcription
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: positively regulates ALD1 and FMO1 expression and promotes early Pip accumulation during pathogen infection
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SARD1 directly targets promoters of ALD1 and FMO1 as well as ICS1, EDS1 and PAD4. 2. sard1 cbp60g double mutants show attenuated ALD1 and FMO1 expression and reduced early Pip accumulation. 3. SARD1 contributes to transcriptional activation of the NHP biosynthetic pathway during SAR.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: biosynthesis of Pip/NHP and linked SA pathway activation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates ALD1 and FMO1 expression; promotes broader defense gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: important positive transcriptional activator of NHP biosynthetic genes during pathogen induction
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: participates in positive defense amplification loop linked to Pip/NHP and SA
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ChIP-seq; mutant analysis; gene expression analysis; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising regulator for coordinated activation of NHP and SA pathways, though broad defense activation may create fitness costs.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: growth-defense tradeoffs are likely upon constitutive immune activation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts together with CBP60g; highlighted by the review as a TF regulating the pathway.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: activates systemic acquired resistance and associated immune transcription
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: positively regulates ALD1 and FMO1 expression and promotes early Pip accumulation during pathogen infection
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CBP60g targets promoters of ALD1 and FMO1 alongside ICS1, EDS1 and PAD4. 2. sard1 cbp60g double mutants display attenuated ALD1/FMO1 induction and reduced early Pip accumulation. 3. CBP60g contributes to transcriptional activation of the NHP biosynthetic pathway during SAR.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: plant-specific ACBP60 family transcription factor; calmodulin-binding protein
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: biosynthesis of Pip/NHP and linked SA pathway activation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: important positive transcriptional activator of NHP biosynthetic genes during pathogen induction
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: participates in positive defense amplification loop
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ChIP-seq; mutant analysis; gene expression analysis; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for coordinated enhancement of immune metabolite production, but likely pleiotropic if constitutively activated.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: likely growth-defense tradeoffs under constitutive activation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functions redundantly/overlapping with SARD1 in pathway regulation.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: activates systemic acquired resistance and immune transcription
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes SARD1/CBP60g expression and supports early pathogen-induced Pip accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TGA1 promotes expression of SARD1 and CBP60g. 2. ChIP-PCR indicated that TGA1 binds the SARD1 promoter. 3. tga1 tga4 mutants accumulate reduced levels of Pip during early bacterial infection, linking TGA1 to NHP pathway regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: transcriptional control of NHP biosynthesis via upstream defense TF cascade
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: upstream positive regulator contributing to early activation of the NHP pathway
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: pathogen infection; NPR1-associated SA signaling context
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: feedforward activation through SARD1/CBP60g toward ALD1/FMO1
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: interacts with NPR1 in SA signaling context as known from cited literature
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ChIP-PCR; double-mutant analysis; metabolite profiling; gene expression analysis
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential lever for coordinated activation of SAR TF cascades, though broad SA-defense interactions may complicate precise engineering.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: possible growth-defense costs from constitutive immune transcription
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The review emphasizes joint action of TGA1/4 with SARD1/CBP60g.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: activates systemic acquired resistance and immune transcription
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: acts with TGA1 to promote SARD1/CBP60g expression and early Pip accumulation during infection
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TGA4 acts together with TGA1 upstream of SARD1/CBP60g. 2. tga1 tga4 double mutants accumulate less Pip during early infection. 3. TGA4 therefore contributes to transcriptional activation of the NHP biosynthetic pathway.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: transcriptional control of NHP biosynthesis via upstream defense TF cascade
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: promotes SARD1/CBP60g expression together with TGA1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: upstream positive regulator contributing to early activation of the NHP pathway
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: pathogen infection; NPR1-associated SA signaling context
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: feedforward activation through SARD1/CBP60g toward ALD1/FMO1
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: functions with TGA1; NPR1-associated TGA family context
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: double-mutant analysis; metabolite profiling; gene expression analysis
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible upstream regulator for coordinated defense enhancement, but with broad immune effects.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: possible growth-defense costs from constitutive immune transcription
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence in the review is mainly from the tga1 tga4 double mutant context.
